programs.name: Idaho Medicaid for the Aged, Blind, and Disabled (ABD)
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical coverage for eligible seniors and disabled individuals, including long-term care services. It covers doctor visits, hospital stays, prescription drugs, and other health needs for those who qualify financially.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals with low income and limited assets meeting Idaho's Medicaid ABD eligibility criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by services; covers eligible medical costs including nursing home care and home health.

programs.name: Idaho Aged and Disabled Waiver
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services to help seniors and disabled individuals avoid institutionalization. Services include personal care, homemaker assistance, and adult day health to support independent living.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled adults at risk of nursing home placement with income/assets meeting Medicaid waiver limits.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; up to $50,000+ annually in services depending on assessed needs.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) - Limited Availability in Idaho
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day care and transportation. It coordinates all Medicare and Medicaid-covered care through interdisciplinary teams.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ certified as nursing home eligible who live in PACE service areas (not widely available in ID).
programs.benefit_value: Full coverage of approved services; no additional premiums for dual-eligible participants.

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: Pays Medicare premiums, deductibles, and copayments for low-income beneficiaries. QMB covers Part A/B premiums and costs; SLMB covers Part B; QI offers additional Part B help.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors on Medicare with incomes below 100-135% FPL and limited assets (administered via Idaho Medicaid).
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Part A/B cost coverage; SLMB/QI: Part B premium ($174.70/month in 2024).

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card for purchasing groceries at approved retailers. Seniors get deductions for medical expenses over $35/month to increase eligibility.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors 60+ with net income under 100% FPL ($1,580/month gross for individual).
programs.benefit_value: Up to $291/month max for individual; average $105-125/month for seniors 60+.

programs.name: Idaho Energy Assistance (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers grants to help pay heating and cooling bills during winter months. Reduces energy costs for low-income households through direct payments to utilities.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (typically <150% FPL) during October-March application period.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Improves home energy efficiency through insulation, sealing leaks, and efficient appliances/heaters. Free services reduce utility bills and improve health/safety.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors (often <200% FPL) and disabled homeowners or renters.
programs.benefit_value: Free home upgrades; average energy savings 20-30% on bills.

programs.name: Senior Health Insurance Benefit Advisors (SHIBA) Program
programs.what_it_does: SHIBA offers free, statewide counseling, information, and referrals on Medicare, Medicaid, and long-term care insurance choices. It assists seniors in navigating health insurance options and resolving related issues.[6]
programs.who_its_for: All Idaho residents, particularly seniors dealing with Medicare, Medicaid, or long-term care insurance.[6]
programs.whats_unique: Idaho Department of Insurance-run program providing unbiased, expert assistance on senior health insurance, not a standard federal offering and varies by state implementation.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Free counseling services; potential savings on insurance premiums through informed choices, no fixed monetary benefit.[6]

programs.name: Meals on Wheels (via Area Agencies on Aging)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to combat malnutrition and isolation. Often includes safety checks and friendly visits by volunteers.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors 60+ unable to prepare meals, often on low income via AAAs.

programs.name: Family Caregiver Support Program (FCSP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers respite care, training, and support groups to ease caregiver burden. Provides supplemental services like personal care to enable family caregiving.
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors 60+ or disabled adults via Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; respite up to $20-30/hour, training free.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides part-time paid work training at community sites leading to unsubsidized jobs. Combines employment with supportive services for older workers.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income seniors 55+ seeking work experience.
programs.benefit_value: Minimum wage part-time work (~20 hrs/week); training and job placement.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Seniors (via AAAs)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free legal advice, representation for benefits, housing, and abuse issues. Helps navigate Medicare, Medicaid, and elder rights matters.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors 60+ through Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.benefit_value: Free consultations and representation; value varies by case.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in long-term care facilities resolving complaints and ensuring quality care. Provides confidential assistance and education on rights.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors in nursing homes, assisted living, or similar facilities in Idaho.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy services; protects rights and improves facility conditions.

programs.name: LEARN Idaho Free Senior Education Program
programs.what_it_does: LEARN Idaho provides free education resources including video libraries, in-person tech classes, online courses, and community events focused on caregiving, technology, health, and digital literacy for seniors. It empowers older adults statewide through accessible, no-cost lifelong learning tailored to regional needs.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Idaho residents aged 60 and older, caregivers, and professionals.[1]
programs.whats_unique: Statewide nonprofit offering free senior-specific tech education and elder care video libraries not universally available in other states; partners with universities like Boise State for fee-waived class audits.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Free access to video libraries, in-person classes, online modules, and events; no monetary value specified, fully no-cost.[1]
